reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify New York counties with at least 65 bridges rated Poor.
2. From those counties, keep the ones with at least 50 fire departments.
3. From the remaining counties, keep the ones with at least 100 bank ATMs.
4. From the remaining counties, keep the ones with at least 1.0 MW of operational renewable energy capacity.
5. For the counties kept after the four filters, determine each county seat for later use.
6. For those same counties, compare 2021 violent-crime counts and keep the county with the higher count.
7. Using the county-seat information for the county selected in step 6, determine the land company that developed that seat.
8. Using the land company identified in step 7, return its founding year.
original_final_question: Among NY counties with at least 65 bridges rated 'Poor' according to the department of transportation, contains at least 50 fire departments, at least 100 bank ATMs, and at least 1.0 MW of operational renewable energy capacity. Of these counties, focus on the county with more violent crimes in 2021. In what year was the land company founded that developed this county's seat? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=4, data.gov intersection - nodes 1-4 all independent):
Node 1: Bridge conditions - NY counties with >=65 Poor bridges -> {Ulster (84), Erie (82), Westchester (68), Orange (67)}
Node 2: Fire departments - NY counties with >=50 fire depts -> {Suffolk (108), Erie (91), Nassau (70), Westchester (57), Onondaga (53), Orange (52), Ulster (51), Oneida (50)}

Node 3: Bank ATMs - NY counties with >=100 ATMs -> {NY (676), Nassau (515), Suffolk (494), Queens (429), Kings (388), Westchester (335), Erie (244), Monroe (232), Onondaga (182), Bronx (158), Albany (148), Orange (128), Dutchess (125), Richmond (115)}
Node 4: Renewable energy - NY counties with >=1.0 MW operational -> {Steuben (286.29), Chautauqua (185.90), Lewis (183.99), ..., Erie (11.48), ..., Westchester (2.64), ...}
Note: Ulster excluded by Node 3 (76 ATMs < 100) and Node 4 (0 MW renewable); Orange excluded by Node 4 (0 MW renewable)
HOP 2 (d=2, Wikipedia - nodes 5-6 BOTH depend on Hop 1 intersection):
Node 5: Erie County Wikipedia -> county seat is Buffalo
Node 6: Westchester County Wikipedia -> county seat is White Plains
Result: {Buffalo, White Plains} - the county seats of the two qualifying counties
HOP 3 (d=1, data.gov comparative - node 7 compares both counties from SAME dataset):
Node 7: Index crimes by county 2021 -> Erie (3,493) vs Westchester (1,520)
Comparative: 3,493 > 1,520 -> Erie has more violent crimes -> Erie County selected
HOP 4 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 8 depends on Hop 3 comparative result):
Node 8: Buffalo Wikipedia -> Holland Land Company developed the area around 1800
HOP 5 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 9 depends on node 8):
Node 9: Holland Land Company Wikipedia -> founded in 1789
